## {{item.title}}

# 8 Best Tools for Data Analysts

![](/content/dam/wgu-65-assets/web-sites/author-headshots/new-linsi-tuttle-pic.jpg)
![](https://www.wgu.edu/blog/2023/10/11-best-tools-for-data-analysts/_jcr_content/root/container/imageandtext.coreimg.jpeg/1701987639143/data.jpeg)

## Data Analytics Tools List

Data analysis is a dynamic, rapidly evolving field involving an ever-growing range of tools and technologies. From AI-driven statistical modeling to real-time business intelligence solutions, data analysis tools provide innovative methods for uncovering correlations, predicting trends, and making data-informed decisions. The newest data analysis solutions leverage cutting-edge technologies such as machine learning, artificial intelligence, and natural language processing to enhance analytical processes and give businesses a competitive edge. Users can easily view, sort, evaluate, and visualize big data, empowering them to optimize workflows, mitigate risks, understand market fluctuations, and identify growth opportunities.

Whether you’re preparing for a job interview, deciding on a degree program, changing careers, or simply expanding your skill set, staying up to date on the latest data analysis technologies is essential. So, what are the best data analytics tools? Keep reading to explore some of the leading data analysis programs and discover their unique advantages, features, and applications.

## Tableau

Established in 2003, [Tableau](https://www.tableau.com/) offers a streamlined data visualization process that’s accessible to both seasoned professionals and beginners. Its robust analytics features make it an indispensable tool for evaluating performance metrics, identifying patterns, and generating forecasts. Users can import and organize information from multiple sources, including databases, spreadsheets, cloud services, and applications. They can then filter data to isolate areas of interest. The integrated visualization tools make it easy to combine data from multiple sources and create interactive illustrations that convey essential information through charts, graphs, maps, and diagrams.

#### Key Features of Tableau

## Why Tableau is Essential for Data Analysts

Tableau is a powerful data management and analysis tool that offers an extensive array of features for effectively handling, organizing, and examining data. With its user-friendly interface and intuitive drag-and-drop functions, even inexperienced users can gain crucial insights. The stories and dashboard features equip data analysts with powerful tools for communicating information to stakeholders in a clear, concise, and engaging way. Tableau’s responsive layout automatically adjusts to different screen sizes and resolutions, meaning an analyst can access data on their desktop and seamlessly switch to a smartphone or other device without losing functionality. Learn more about Tableau by visiting WGU’s [Tableau programming page](https://www.wgu.edu/online-it-degrees/programming-languages/tableau.html).

## R

[R](https://www.r-project.org/) is an innovative open-source programming language that allows users to manipulate, analyze, and visualize data in ways that traditional tools cannot. With R, users can import data from various sources, create custom calculations, and build complex visualizations that help them gain deeper insights. It can efficiently handle large data sets, which makes it an essential tool for users who manage dense, complex information. R boasts a comprehensive package library for a wide range of statistical and graphical functions, including linear and nonlinear modeling, time-series analysis, classical statistical tests, and clustering.

#### Key Features of R

## Why R is Essential for Data Analysts

This programming language has gained widespread popularity among data analysts due to its extensive statistical analysis, data manipulation, and data visualization capabilities. The predictive modeling features allow analysts to forecast future trends, identify patterns, and optimize decision-making, empowering them to take a proactive approach to organizational growth and development. R also contains reporting tools that transform code into visually appealing, dynamic illustrations. These features allow analysts to clearly convey data-powered findings to stakeholders who don’t have a background in data science. Because R is open-source, users can utilize the language at no cost and partner with the growing R community. Continue exploring R’s unique data analysis tools by visiting WGU’s [R programming page](https://www.wgu.edu/online-it-degrees/programming-languages/r-programming.html).

## Python

[Python](https://www.python.org/) is quickly becoming the go-to programming language for all things data. As an open-source language, it's free to use, flexible, and constantly improved upon by a massive community of developers. Its popularity is due in part to its simplicity. Even those who are new to programming can get a grasp of Python's syntax relatively quickly. However, it's not just beginner-friendly; it's powerful and robust and has specialized libraries for everything from data analysis and visualization to machine learning.

#### Key Features of Python

## Why Python is Essential for Data Analysts

Because Python is so widely used, learning Python can be a crucial step toward career advancement in data analysis. The program’s versatility, integration capabilities, and expansive libraries make it a valuable tool for data analysts working in a variety of sectors, including finance, healthcare, retail, manufacturing, and telecommunications. Many refer to Python as the “Swiss Army Knife of programming languages.” Python enables data scientists and analysts to perform complex explorations and visualizations quickly and easily. This programming language can also automate repetitive data processing tasks, saving companies time and money. By mastering Python, data analysts learn in-demand skills that apply to various lucrative careers. Check out WGU’s [Python programming skills](https://www.wgu.edu/skills-guides/python-programming.html) resource to learn more.

## Excel

Individuals and businesses worldwide use [Microsoft Excel](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/excel). This user-friendly spreadsheet program allows individuals to store, organize, filter, and analyze data in a clean, simple interface. Excel’s features range from basic calculations to sophisticated data transformation formulas, making it a valuable tool for rudimentary data storage as well as in-depth analysis. Users can create pivot tables, charts, graphs, scatter plots, histograms, and other visual representations to summarize and illustrate data. This is especially useful for sharing complex information in a clear and understandable manner. Organizations across industries leverage Excel to create custom reports and budgets, plan projects, track inventory, analyze sales data, visualize trends, and share metrics with stakeholders. With a user base of over [750 million people](https://financialpost.com/personal-finance/business-essentials/63-per-cent-of-companies-consider-excel-a-vital-accounting-tool), it’s widely recognized as the most popular spreadsheet program in the world.

#### Key Features of Excel

## Why Excel is Essential for Data Analysts

Excel is an indispensable resource for data analysts. While other software programs are more robust, few applications match Excel’s ease of use, intuitive design, and versatility. Many data analysts use Excel’s pivot tables to instantaneously count, add, subtract, or average numerical data. They leverage graphs and charts to summarize dense information and highlight crucial details. Through conditional formatting, data analysts reveal trends, detect outliers, compare data sets, and enhance data clarity. Excel’s impressive integration capabilities allow users to import data from other programs, databases, text files, and web queries smoothly.

## Power BI

[Power BI](https://app.powerbi.com/home?experience=power-bi) is a Microsoft product offering business intelligence and data management solutions for a variety of purposes. It’s currently one of the most widely used business analytics platforms, with [97%](https://corporatefinanceinstitute.com/resources/business-intelligence/top-10-power-bi-features/) of Fortune 500 companies utilizing the tool for everything from data visualizations to report deployment. The program incorporates robust data transformation and analysis tools that allow users to create relationships between data sets, perform complex statistical analyses, and forecast future trends. Additionally, Power BI’s built-in artificial intelligence feature automatically identifies patterns, correlations, and variances in data, reducing the need for manual analysis. The program offers exceptional data visualization capabilities. Leveraging an intuitive drag-and-drop interface, users can create customized dashboards that combine data from numerous reports into a cohesive graphic. One of Power BI’s most unique attributes is the Q&A feature that enables users to ask questions about data in plain language, providing a fast, convenient way to access specific information.

#### Key Features of Power BI

## Why Power BI is Essential for Data Analysts

Power BI is an essential tool for data analysts because it provides a comprehensive suite of data visualization and reporting features unparalleled in the market. With Power BI, analysts can quickly and easily access all their information sources, transform data, and create custom visualizations that are easy to understand, explain, and share with stakeholders. Additionally, Power BI is highly customizable, so analysts can configure it to meet their organization’s specific needs. Navigate to WGU’s [Power BI](https://www.wgu.edu/online-it-degrees/programming-languages/power-bi.html) resource to learn more.

## Apache Spark

[Apache Spark](https://spark.apache.org/) originated as a collaborative research project at UC Berkeley and has since become one of the most widely used big data processing software programs available. This unified analytics program enables developers, analysts, and engineers to work with both batch and streaming data processing within a single codebase. Its in-memory processing significantly enhances performance, allowing faster data access and analysis than other technologies. Additionally, Apache Spark supports several programming languages, including Python, Java, and Scala, making it suitable for users with varied backgrounds. It’s a popular tool in many industries, including finance, e-commerce, healthcare, marketing, and entertainment.

#### Key Features of Apache Spark

## Why Apache Spark is Essential for Data Analysts

Data analysts who need to process massive amounts of data quickly and efficiently frequently choose Apache Spark due to its processing speed, versatility, and integration capabilities. Unlike traditional data processing tools, Apache Spark can be used for both batch processing and real-time streaming. With Spark, analysts can easily manipulate and transform data, create complex machine learning models, and run sophisticated analytics without worrying about performance issues or limitations. This means that data analysts can enhance the speed and accuracy of their investigations, which is essential for conducting robust and insightful data analyses.

## Talend

[Talend](https://www.talend.com/) is a powerful and versatile data integration platform that helps businesses improve operational efficiency and decision-making. With its user-friendly interface and intuitive tools, organizations can map and transform data, automate complex workflows, view real-time trends, and generate actionable insights. Talend offers various connectors and adapters, allowing users to seamlessly integrate data from cloud-based applications, legacy systems, and social media platforms. Ultimately, Talend empowers organizations to make faster, better-informed decisions and stay ahead of the competition in today's data-driven world.

#### Key Features of Talend

## Why Talend is Essential for Data Analysts

Data analysts and scientists interpret complex data and provide valuable insights to business decision-makers. However, the sheer volume and diversity of data from multiple sources can make this task overwhelming. This is where Talend comes into play. Talend's integration platform streamlines and simplifies data governance and data integration, ensuring that data integrity and quality are maintained throughout the entire process. By leveraging impressive computing power and versatile features, this tool can meet a wide range of dynamic needs.

## Splunk

[Splunk](https://www.splunk.com/en_us/download.html?utm_campaign=google_amer_en_search_brand&utm_source=google&utm_medium=cpc&utm_content=free_trials_downloads&utm_term=splunk&device=c&_bt=662330344234&_bm=e&_bn=g&gad_source=1&gclid=Cj0KCQjw6auyBhDzARIsALIo6v-gQBHdPuCCRKY1vg50HXRPbUXk_UZ4hZmNKOdFIgyq6iC8yCAbAl8aAooMEALw_wcB) is a powerful analytics platform that helps organizations make sense of their data. It's designed to handle massive amounts of information from numerous sources, including servers, mobile devices, and applications. With its comprehensive search capabilities, Splunk allows users to find the insights they need to make informed decisions. It's a versatile tool that can be used for a variety of purposes, including IT, cybersecurity, business intelligence, human resources, and marketing.

#### Key Features of Splunk

## Why Splunk is Essential for Data Analysts

Splunk offers a comprehensive platform for gathering, storing, and analyzing information. Utilizing real-time data, analysts can immediately discover patterns and detect anomalies. Splunk’s search feature helps analysts find specific information without manually sifting through large quantities of data. The platform boasts many advanced analytics capabilities, including natural language processing, machine learning, and predictive modeling, that enhance data scientists' ability to gain actionable insights from the data. Splunk also includes visualization tools for clearly expressing complex data.

## Things to Consider When Selecting a Data Analysis Tool

When choosing a data analytics tool, consider the following factors:

## Advance Your Career by Mastering Data Analytics Software Tools

In our information-driven world, data analytics is becoming increasingly vital across all industries. According to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS), the number of job openings for data analysts is expected to grow by [23%](https://www.bls.gov/ooh/math/operations-research-analysts.htm#tab-6) from 2022 to 2032, while the openings for data scientists will increase by [35%](https://www.bls.gov/ooh/math/data-scientists.htm#tab-6). The [demand for data analysts and scientists](https://www.forbes.com/sites/bernhardschroeder/2021/06/11/the-data-analytics-profession-and-employment-is-exploding-three-trends-that-matter/) is skyrocketing, making now the perfect time to enter or advance in this lucrative field.

WGU’s [Bachelor of Science in Data Analytics](https://www.wgu.edu/online-it-degrees/data-analytics-bachelors-program.html) degree program teaches you the cutting-edge data analytics software tools and industry skills you need to thrive. From data management to model deployment and storytelling, this degree covers the essential proficiencies employers seek. Our B.S. in Data Analytics also incorporates two WGU data analytics certificates, Data Analyst Practitioner and Data Engineering Practitioner. If you’re ready to take your data analysis skills to the next level, the WGU [Master’s in Data Analytics](https://www.wgu.edu/online-it-degrees/data-analytics-masters-program.html) delves deeper into advanced data science and analysis. It includes WGU data analytics certificates in Data Preparation, Data Analytics Fundamentals, and Advanced Data Modeling. WGU’s courses are fully online, meaning you can study when and where you want. Additionally, our competency-based learning model empowers you to advance through courses as soon as you show mastery of the material so you can graduate sooner, potentially saving time and money. Whether you’re a seasoned data analyst or just beginning your career, a degree from WGU can unlock a wealth of opportunities and play an integral part in your career journey.

Ready to Start Your Journey?

# Big Data Analytics: Techniques, Benefits, and Best Practices for Reliable Data

![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/64fef88ee8b22d3d21b715a2/674089cb8250b7e965bbea86_Big%20Data%20Analytics_%20Techniques%2C%20Benefits%2C%20and%20Best%20Practices%20for%20Reliable%20Data%20%20.webp)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/plugins/Basic/assets/placeholder.60f9b1840c.svg)

Every minute, the world generates [**463 exabytes of data**](https://www.statista.com/statistics/871513/worldwide-data-created/)âenough to stream 92 million HD movies at the same time. Businesses today arenât struggling to find data; theyâre struggling to make sense of it. The real challenge is turning all that information into clear insights that drive smart decisions and real results.

**Big data analytics** helps companies solve this problem. Itâs the tool that turns raw data into actionsâhelping predict trends, cut costs, and create better customer experiences. But this doesnât happen automatically. Many businesses face big hurdles, like keeping [data clean](https://www.acceldata.io/blog/cleaning-your-data-swamp-with-data-observability), finding the right tools, and scaling their systems as data grows.

This article will break down how to get it right: the key techniques, tools, and real-world strategies behind effective big data analytics.

## What Is Big Data Analytics?Â Â

**Big data analytics** involves analyzing massive datasetsâstructured, semi-structured, and unstructuredâto uncover patterns, trends, and actionable insights.Â

By processing information from sources like IoT sensors, social media, and cloud systems, big data analytics enables smarter decisions and innovation.

For instance, [Spotify](https://exchange.scale.com/public/blogs/inside-the-content-recommendation-engine-at-the-heart-of-spotify) processes billions of daily user interactionsâsearches, skips, and playlistsâto deliver personalized recommendations, keeping users engaged. At its core, big data analytics transforms overwhelming information into valuable insights, driving efficiency and growth.Â

## Key Components of Big Data AnalyticsÂ Â

Turning raw data into real results requires mastering each step in the process. From collecting information to creating clear visuals, every stage is key to making data work for your business.

## Types of Big Data AnalyticsÂ Â

Big data analytics comes in four distinct forms, each designed to answer specific questions. From understanding past performance to forecasting future trends and prescribing the best actions, these methods provide the clarity businesses need to act decisively.

| Type | Description | Where Itâs Useful | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Descriptive Analytics | Explains âwhat happenedâ by summarizing past trends. | Understanding historical performance and trends. | A clothing retailer analyzes last seasonâs sales to decide which styles to restock. |
| Diagnostic Analytics | Focuses on âwhyâ something occurred by identifying root causes. | Addressing recurring problems or anomalies. | An e-commerce store finds high shipping costs are the reason for abandoned carts. |
| Predictive Analytics | Forecasts âwhat could happenâ using past and current data. | Anticipating trends, risks, and opportunities. | [PayPal](https://safebreach.paypal.com/us/security/learn-about-paypal-secure-technology) flags potentially fraudulent transactions before they are completed. |
| Prescriptive Analytics | Recommends âwhat to do nextâ based on predictive insights. | Optimizing strategies and maximizing outcomes. | [Airlines](https://www.price2spy.com/blog/dynamic-pricing-in-the-airline-industry/) adjust ticket prices dynamically based on demand, weather, and fuel costs. |

## Benefits of Big Data AnalyticsÂ Â

The debate around **'big data vs. analytics'** often highlights the ability of big data analytics to uncover trends in unstructured and semi-structured data, offering businesses insights that traditional methods can't match. Big data analytics empowers businesses to make smarter decisions, improve customer relationships, streamline operations, and manage risks effectively.

## Big Data Analytics Techniques and Algorithms

Big data analytics relies on advanced techniques to process and derive insights from vast datasets. These methods turn complex information into actionable intelligence.

## Big Data Analytics Tools and Platforms

The right tools form the backbone of big data analytics, enabling organizations to store, process, and visualize massive datasets efficiently. Hereâs a breakdown of big data analytics tools, their applications, and examples:

| Category | Tool | Key Features | Best For | Implementation Tip |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Core Processing | [Hadoop](https://hadoop.apache.org/) | Distributed storage, parallel processing | Batch processing for data lakes (e.g., analyzing historical sales data). | Start with a small cluster and scale as needed |
|  | [Apache Spark](https://spark.apache.org/) | In-memory processing, stream handling | Real-time analytics (e.g., processing GPS data for live route optimization). | Use Sparkâs MLlib for predictive tasks |
| Cloud Solutions | [Google BigQuery](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery) | Serverless architecture, automatic scaling | Ad-hoc analysis (e.g., running queries on massive marketing datasets). | Use partitioned tables to optimize costs |
|  | Amazon Redshift | Columnar storage, parallel processing | Enterprise BI (e.g., analyzing clinical trial data for research insights). | Enable automatic workload management |
| Visualization | Tableau | Drag-and-drop interface, interactive dashboards | Interactive reporting (e.g., visualizing inventory trends for decision-making). | Use data extracts for faster dashboard performance |
|  | [Power BI](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/products/power-bi) | Microsoft ecosystem integration, AI insights | Dynamic reports (e.g., monitoring real-time traffic data for operations). | Leverage DirectQuery for live data needs |
| Specialized Tools | [TensorFlow](https://www.tensorflow.org/) | Deep learning, neural networks, GPU support | Advanced AI (e.g., building predictive models for user behavior). | Use pre-built models for common ML tasks |
|  | [Acceldata](https://www.acceldata.io/) | [Data observability](https://www.acceldata.io/why-data-observability),[quality monitoring](https://www.acceldata.io/article/what-is-data-quality-management), real-time performance optimization | Ensuring clean,[reliable pipelines](https://www.acceldata.io/blog/master-data-pipelines-why-slas-are-your-key-to-success) (e.g., identifying and fixing bottlenecks in data operations). | Leverage Acceldata for monitoring across multiple systems to enhance analytics reliability. |

## Real-World Applications of Big Data Analytics

Big data analytics is reshaping industries by enabling smarter decisions and better outcomes. Hereâs how companies across sectors use it:

## Best Practices for Effective Big Data AnalyticsÂ Â

To leverage big data fully, companies must follow proven practices that ensure accuracy, scalability, and impact. Here are five best practices with real-world examples:

## Addressing Common Big Data Analytics Challenges

Big data analytics has the power to drive innovation, but itâs not without obstacles. From securing sensitive information to handling ever-growing datasets, success requires proactive solutions to common big data analytics challenges.

## Enhancing Big Data Analytics with AcceldataÂ Â

Addressing the challenges of big data analytics requires solutions that ensure [data quality](https://www.acceldata.io/blog/quality-data-or-just-data), scalability, and reliability. Many organizations struggle with fragmented data systems, escalating operational costs, and the need for consistent performance at scale. This is where platforms like [Acceldata](https://www.acceldata.io/) come in, offering a comprehensive suite of tools to tackle these challenges effectively.  
  
For example, [PubMatic](https://www.acceldata.io/blog/pubmatic-leverages-data-observability-platform), a leading digital advertising provider, leveraged Acceldataâs real-time monitoring and data quality assurance to reduce its HDFS block footprint by 30% and save over $2 million annually in OEM licensing costs. Acceldataâs ability to optimize data operations and enhance system performance makes it a critical tool for organizations seeking to maximize the value of their big data initiatives.

Take the next stepâ[book a demo with Acceldata](https://www.acceldata.io/demo) to see how it can transform your data strategy.

## SummaryÂ Â

Big Data Analytics empowers organizations to turn vast amounts of information into actionable insights, driving smarter decisions, personalized customer experiences, and operational efficiency. By leveraging the right tools, addressing challenges like data quality and scalability, and adopting best practices, businesses can maximize the value of their data. Solutions like Acceldata simplify this process, offering real-time monitoring and quality assurance to ensure seamless and reliable analytics operations.

# 7 Top Data Analytics Tools Every Data Analyst Should Master

*[7 Top Data Analytics Tools Every Data Analyst Should Master](https://www.springboard.com/blog/data-analytics/7-top-data-analytics-tools-every-data-analyst-should-master/) was originally published on [Springboard](https://www.springboard.com/blog).*

With data becoming a major asset for many organizations, data analysts need tools that help interpret the information. Having the right data analytics tools makes it easier to come up with creative ways to derive valuable insights from a company’s various data sources.**‌**

## **What Are Data Analytics Tools?**

Data analytics tools are programs and software platforms used by data analysts to collect, store, analyze, and distribute actionable information derived from company data. This typically includes information about customers, competitors, and even workers within the organization. Having the right tools helps data analysts format information to develop more effective business strategies.

## **Importance of Data Analytics Tools**

Data analysts often have to work with qualitative and quantitative data. Qualitative data comes from sources like surveys, email responses, and social media. Quantitative data is typically stored in databases and spreadsheets. Data analytics tools make it easier to work with both types of information. Data analysis tools help you mine big data more quickly and effectively, helping to find answers to specific business questions.**‌**

## **7 Data Analytics Tools You Need in Your Armour**

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/springboard-images/image/upload/w_1080,c_limit,q_auto,f_auto,fl_lossy/wordpress/2021/06/7-Data-Analytics-Tools-Every-Data-Analyst-Should-Master-1.png)

![](https://res.cloudinary.com/springboard-images/image/upload/w_1080,c_limit,q_auto,f_auto,fl_lossy/wordpress/2021/06/7-Data-Analytics-Tools-Every-Data-Analyst-Should-Master-1.png)

#### **1. Tableau**

[Tableau](https://www.tableau.com/) is a [data visualization](https://www.springboard.com/blog/data-analytics/springboard-tutorial-tableau/) tool that helps you simplify the presentation of raw data, making it more understandable when shared with other professionals within your organization. Data analysts like using Tableau because it doesn’t require special technical knowledge or programming skills to get started.

Companies making use of Tableau include [Cisco, LinkedIn, and Lenovo](https://www.tableau.com/solutions/high-technology-analytics).

The platform provides [free training videos](https://www.tableau.com/learn/training/20211) to help you start preparing, analyzing, and sharing data. You can display your Tableau skills by connecting to a data warehouse and creating live dashboards that provide a real-time view of data for different business areas.

Tableau Public is a free version of Tableau that allows you to connect to spreadsheets, files, and web data resources like Google Sheets to build data visualizations in an interface. Individual Tableau licenses currently cost $70 per month and let you connect to databases and other private data sources. Organizations can reach out to Tableau to get pricing for group licensing.

#### 2. **Microsoft Power BI**

[Power BI](https://powerbi.microsoft.com/en-us/) is a platform of cloud-based business intelligence applications and services. Data analysts use it to pull large quantities of information from various sources, including Excel, and transform data to create visuals that help companies make sense of the data.

Power BI is popular among data analysts because they can create data models and reports to share within an organization.

Businesses creating solutions with Power BI include [Hitachi Solutions](https://customers.microsoft.com/en-us/story/753401-hitachi-vantara-project-online-partner-professional-services), [Adobe](https://customers.microsoft.com/en-us/story/adobe-azure-dynamics-power-bi), and [Toyota](https://customers.microsoft.com/en-us/story/763052-toyota-motor-north-america-automotive-power-apps).

Microsoft provides [free learning resources](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/users/microsoftpowerplatform-5978/collections/djwu3eywpk4nm) for data analysts interested in learning how to use the platform and eventually becoming certified. You can showcase your Power BI skills by creating reports and visualizations from the information pulled from different data resources, then distributing them to business stakeholders.

Power BI Desktop is a free data analysis and report creation tool for Windows computers. You can use the platform to create interactive visuals, then share them through an organization’s Power BI Service. Pro licenses for Power BI start at $9.99 per month per user.

#### **3. SAS**

[SAS](https://www.sas.com/en_us/home.html) (Statistical Analysis Software) is a software suite used to retrieve, analyze, and report statistical data. Data analysts use SAS to extract data from databases and raw data files to identify and analyze various data patterns that have meaning for the company. It’s popular among data analysts because SAS requires no other special technical skills besides some SQL familiarity.

Organizations that use SAS include [Honda](https://www.sas.com/en_us/customers/american-honda.html), [Kia](https://www.sas.com/en_us/customers/kia-motors-america.html), and [Office Depot](https://www.sas.com/en_us/customers/office-depot-europe.html).

The company provides [free tutorials](https://video.sas.com/detail/videos/how-to-tutorials) and paid training to help you learn the platform. You can use SAS with projects that involve looking back at historical business data to help companies figure out possible future trends within an industry. SAS offers free trials and demos to organizations interested in using the software to tailor it to fit the industry.

#### **4. Erwin**

The [E](https://erwin.com/)[rwin](https://erwin.com/) platform includes data modeling tools used by data analysts to map out the details of databases within an organization. It’s useful for data analysts who need to reverse-engineer an existing database to get a sense of the information it contains.

Companies currently using Erwin include [Fidelity International](https://www.erwin.com/casestudy/fidelity-international8148028/) and [CenturyLink](https://www.erwin.com/casestudy/centurylink8148012/).

The company offers paid [online an](https://erwin.com/services/erwin-product-training/)d instructor-led courses to organizations looking to train data analysts on using the tool. You can also use Erwin for projects requiring visualizations of existing data models and data dictionaries.

Companies can request a free demo of Erwin or purchase licenses by contacting a company sales representative.

#### **5. SQL Server Integration Services**

[SQL Server Integration Services](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/integration-services/sql-server-integration-services?view=sql-server-ver15) (SSIS) is an Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL) tool used to extract data from sources like Excel, Hadoop, and SQL databases. Analysts rely on the tool to design workflows, perform data integration tasks, and automate data extraction needed for different analysis projects.

Microsoft provides free tutorials to walk you through how to create and distribute SSIS packages. You can use SSIS for data management when you need to merge data from different sources or for projects that require [data cleaning](https://www.springboard.com/blog/data-science/data-cleaning/) before starting the analysis.

SSIS capabilities come packaged with the purchase of any edition of SQL Server. Organizations using Azure can purchase SSIS as a separate utility.

#### **6. Apache Spark**

[Apache Spark](https://spark.apache.org/) is an open-source data processing analytics engine. Data analysts use it to run queries and processing tasks on large datasets. Many data analysts use Apache Spark because it lets you preprocess a large amount of data at once and read real-time along with stored information.

Companies using Apache Spark include [eBay, Yahoo, and Amazon](https://spark.apache.org/powered-by.html).

The company provides a [getting-started guide](https://spark.apache.org/docs/latest/quick-start.html) and points you to resources that provide more in-depth training. You can use Apache Spark to review unstructured information from customer or product reviews to help organizations spot opportunities to improve the customer experience. Apache Spark is free for anyone to use.

#### 7. **RapidMiner**

[RapidMiner](https://rapidminer.com/) is an integrated data science platform that provides an integrated environment for performing tasks tied to business analytics, [predictive analytics](https://www.springboard.com/blog/data-analytics/what-is-predictive-analytics/), and data mining. Many data analysts use it to make working on projects that require extensive data and text mining easier.

Companies using RapidMiner include [PayPal](https://rapidminer.com/resource/sentiment-analysis-paypal/) and [Domino’s](https://rapidminer.com/resource/r-based-demand-forecasting/).

You can access [free training modules](https://academy.rapidminer.com/) on the RapidMiner website. You can use the platform to look at historical sales data to develop more optimized strategies for targeting promising leads. RapidMiner is available through free trials as both a browser-based and full-scale platform solution.

***Is data analytics the right career for you?***

*Springboard offers a comprehensive data analytics bootcamp. Our data analytics curriculum goes beyond just technical skills to focus on areas where employers find the biggest gaps: strategic thinking, problem-solving, and communication. Watch videos from Microsoft. Learn insights from McKinsey experts. Tackle case studies from Harvard Business School. No other data analytics bootcamp does this. You’ll graduate with an analytical mindset. That’s an edge not just for your job search, but throughout your career.*

# 6 best big data analytics tools to use in 2025

by [Vaishnavi Shah](/author/vaishnavi-shah), Technical Content Writer at ThoughtSpot

Updated Feb 4, 2025

Facing increasingly high customer expectations, new competitive pressures, and an uncertain economic outlook, business leaders need their data to deliver more value than ever before. So, how do you maximize value from data?

Here’s the good news: [Big data analytics](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/analytics/big-data-analytics) tools can empower everyone in your organization to gain meaningful insights from relentless waves of data. In fact, research by [McKinsey](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/growth-marketing-and-sales/our-insights/insights-to-impact-creating-and-sustaining-data-driven-commercial-growth) shows that companies using a data-driven approach report above-market growth and an increase of 15-20% in profits. But with so many options, how do you choose a tool that aligns with your business needs? The answer lies below.

## Top big data analytics tools to explore in 2025

## 1. ThoughtSpot

[ThoughtSpot](https://www.thoughtspot.com/) is a leading intelligence platform that is changing the way businesses make data-driven decisions. The AI-powered, search-driven approach allows you to ask questions in natural language and get instant insights through interactive, drillable visualizations. Whether it’s business users accessing data independently or data teams driving deeper analysis, ThoughtSpot encourages everyone to find the power of their data.

With advanced capabilities designed for true self-service and enterprise-grade security, ThoughtSpot integrates insights easily into your everyday workflows. Your data teams can accelerate their analysis, while you can benefit from AI-powered solutions that deliver real business outcomes.

Take [Wellthy](https://www.thoughtspot.com/resources/case-study/wellthy), for example. By adopting ThoughtSpot, they **saved over $200K by streamlining their analytics pipeline**, freeing up their data team to focus on more strategic work. Partners, customers, and internal teams now access the insights they need with ease, through natural-language search and verified data visualizations

Here’s what Kelly Burdine, Head of Data Science and Analytics, at Wellthy, has to say:

### **Core features of ThoughtSpot:**

#### **1. Spotter, your AI analyst**

[Spotter](https://www.thoughtspot.com/product/ai-analyst) acts as your AI analyst, providing actionable insights in an instant. Whether you ask simple questions like "What were our sales last month?" or dive into more complex queries, Spotter handles it all. Unlike traditional BI tools that need SQL skills, Spotter analyzes data on its own and creates accurate visualizations and reports, making data insights easy for anyone to access.

#### **2. Dynamic dashboards and visualizations**

Next-gen [AI-augmented dashboards](https://www.thoughtspot.com/product/visualize) redefine how you interact with and understand your data. These advanced dashboards provide real-time, interactive insights, allowing you to explore your data as it evolves. Powered by AI, they deliver not just static reports, but dynamic visuals that update instantly as new data comes in.

#### **3. Predictive analytics**

You can forecast future trends and outcomes with this feature. With predictive analytics, you can look beyond historical data and gain a clearer view of what’s coming next. This allows you to proactively plan and make decisions based on data-driven projections, whether it's forecasting sales growth, market shifts, or customer behavior.

#### **4. AI-driven data prep**

You can speed up your [data preparation](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/data-integration/data-preparation-tools) with AI-powered tools that support SQL, Python, and R, making [data cleaning](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/data-science/what-is-data-cleaning-and-how-to-keep-your-data-clean-in-7-steps) and management quicker and more efficient. In [Analyst Studio](https://www.thoughtspot.com/product/analyst-studio), you spend less time manually prepping data and more time analyzing it. The AI-powered tools streamline repetitive tasks, automate processes, and handle complex data sets, freeing you up to focus on deeper insights and strategic analysis.

## 2. Power BI

[Power BI](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/products/power-bi) is a big data analytics tool that integrates with Microsoft’s ecosystem, allowing businesses to analyze and visualize large datasets. The platform offers data analytics features, like data modeling, dashboards, and reporting. Despite its intuitive interface, leveraging Power BI’s advanced capabilities can be challenging, especially for business users who will likely require additional training.

### **Core features of Power BI:**

* **Data visualization and reporting**: Turns data into charts, graphs, and dashboards for easy analysis.
* **Connects with Microsoft ecosystem**: Integrates with tools like Excel, Azure, and SQL Server for data access.
* **Data modeling**: Organizes and structures data for easier analysis, especially when combining multiple sources.
* **Data transformation**: Cleans and reshapes data to prepare it for analysis.
* **Sharing and collaboration**: Allows sharing and collaborating on reports and dashboards with others.

## 3. Qlik Sense

[Qlik Sense](https://www.qlik.com/us/products/qlik-sense) allows business users to run ad-hoc queries and apply advanced data analytics techniques to large datasets. Users can build visualizations, get recommendations, and monitor real-time data. Qlik Sense can be deployed either on-premise or in the cloud.

### **Core features of Qlik Sense:**

* **Data visualization and reporting**: Converts data into interactive visuals for easy insights.
* **Real-time monitoring**: Tracks data as it changes, providing up-to-date information.
* **Data modeling**: Organizes and connects data from different sources for better analysis.
* **Sharing and collaboration**: Facilitates sharing reports and working together on data insights.
* **Embedded analytics**: Integrates analytics directly into apps or workflows for easy use.

## 4. Tableau

[Tableau](https://www.tableau.com/) is Salesforce’s legacy big data analytics tool that lets users visualize and analyze data. With a drag-and-drop interface, users can create [interactive dashboards](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/dashboard/interactive-dashboard) to gain insights. The platform integrates with multiple data sources, allowing you a high-level view of critical KPIs. However, navigating Tableau can be difficult for business users as the platform requires an analytical background.

### **Core features of Tableau:**

* **Data visualization**: Turns data into easy-to-understand visuals.
* **Data connectivity**: Connects to a wide range of data sources for easy access.
* **Available to view on different devices**: Makes it simple to access dashboards on desktop, tablet, or mobile.
* **Report sharing and collaboration**: Allows sharing of reports and collaboration with others.
* **App-level security**: Ensures data is protected with security measures at the app level.

## 5. Apache Hadoop

[Apache Hadoop](https://hadoop.apache.org/) is an open-source framework based on Java that manages the storage and processing of large datasets. Hadoop uses distributed storage and parallel processing to break down enormous amounts of data into smaller workloads, allowing analysts to store and process data quickly.

### **Core features of Apache Hadoop:**

* **Scalable data storage**: Can store large amounts of data across multiple machines.
* **Data processing**: Processes massive datasets using distributed systems.
* **Open-source framework**: Free and open for anyone to use, with a large community for support.
* **Distributed computing model**: Breaks down tasks across multiple machines for faster processing.

## 6. Apache Spark

[Apache Spark](https://spark.apache.org/) is another open-source data-processing big data analytics software that handles large amounts of semi-structured and structured datasets. In addition, the platform leverages in-memory computing to process data and includes several libraries to support SQL queries, stream processing, and AI/ML applications.

### **Core features of Apache Spark:**

* **Stream data processing**: Handles real-time data streams for immediate analysis.
* **Advanced data analytics**: Offers tools for complex analytics, including machine learning.
* **Supports SQL queries**: Allows running SQL queries on large datasets for easy analysis.
* **Open-source framework**: Free to use, with a community for support and development.

## What to look for in a big data analytics tool?

### **1. Handles large volumes of data**

As the name applies, the ability to handle large data volumes is a key requirement for big data analytics tools. That’s because you need a scalable big data analytics solution with real-time processing features to handle your organization’s current and future data volume. Moreover, the platform should support connectivity with multiple databases and [cloud data platforms](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/cloud/cloud-data-platform). This will help you analyze the dormant data in your data warehouses and identify hidden growth opportunities.

### **2. Supports interactive data visualization**

Spreadsheets are boring and quite frankly hard to read. Turning complex datasets into data visualizations helps you present data in a digestible, contextual, and actionable way. As you qualify solutions, ensure that your big data analytics tool has a simple and intuitive user interface that allows users to customize their [dashboards](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/dashboard/what-is-a-dashboard) and create [interactive data visualizations](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/data-visualization/interactive-data-visualization) so everyone can understand data and make timely decisions.

### **3. Features innovative AI capabilities**

Big data analytics tools aren’t limited to running [ad-hoc queries](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/analytics/ad-hoc-analysis-and-reporting/what-is-an-ad-hoc-query) and creating visualizations. [AI-powered big data](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/ai/big-data-ai) solutions offer capabilities like natural language search and machine learning to help business users and [data teams](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/how-to-build-a-modern-data-team-structure-skill-sets-and-common-mistakes) get into the nitty-gritty of data and find valuable insights.

For instance, business users can use AI-powered solutions like ThoughtSpot to ask questions in natural language and get instant AI-assisted answers, visualizations, and recommendations. On the other hand, data teams can leverage conversation AI to dig deeper into their data—asking questions and follow-up questions just as easily as talking to a coworker on Slack. These features significantly reduce time-to-insight and promote timely decisions across the organization.

### **4. Has real-world use cases**

How can you tell if a product is effective in the real world? You have to put it to the test. To establish a vendor’s credibility and demonstrate the solution’s ability to solve complex business problems, check out customer case studies and reviews for up-to-date information about the platform’s capabilities, performance, and customer support. We also recommend exploring the product beforehand through demos, as it will allow you to understand the platform’s capabilities.

### **5. Delivers self-service analytics**

Instead of gatekeeping insights to a select few analysts and IT specialists, your big data analytics tool should deliver [self-service analytics](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/analytics/self-service-analytics). By democratizing data and allowing every business user to find insights on their own, you empower every decision-maker to find the information they need when they need it. With self-service analytics, users can create detailed reports and [live dashboards](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/dashboard/live-dashboard), and monitor business performance on the go, enabling [data-driven decisions](https://www.thoughtspot.com/data-trends/best-practices/data-driven-decision-making) rather than relying on their gut feelings.

## Act now to go from data-aware to data-driven

Data teams spend a lot of time and effort in modeling, transforming, and analyzing data. But if the data doesn’t help business users make timely decisions, you will never be able to drive meaningful growth at scale.

To become a data-driven organization, you need a true self-service data analytics tool that allows business users to ask questions in natural language and build interactive dashboards. That way, your data team isn’t stuck creating hundreds of reports and can focus on larger data initiatives.

ThoughtSpot’s AI-powered search experience empowers business users to visualize real-time data, drill into specific searches, and pin charts to custom Liveboards. With the business know-how and the right tools, your entire organization can make in-the-moment decisions when they count the most.  See how ThoughtSpot reduces time-to-insight—[schedule a demo today](https://www.thoughtspot.com/demo?utm_source=data_trends&utm_medium=seo&utm_term=cta2&utm_content=data_analytics_tools&utm_campaign=dt_analytics).

enriched_points.expanded_content: Hadoop is an open-source framework for distributed storage and processing of large datasets, enabling batch processing and data management in a cost-effective manner.
Apache Spark offers in-memory processing capabilities for real-time data analytics, supporting batch and streaming data to enhance performance and speed in data processing.
Tools like Tableau and Power BI provide interactive interfaces to visualize complex datasets, enabling users to create informative dashboards and reports for better decision-making.
Frameworks such as TensorFlow and Scikit-Learn facilitate the development of predictive models, transforming vast datasets into actionable insights through advanced algorithms and automation.
